pattern: The number of ways to reach a step is the sum of the ways to reach the two steps below it, so the answer is a Fibonacci-style recurrence built bottom-up.
hints: The last move onto step n is either a single step from n-1 or a double step from n-2, and those two routes never overlap.
That makes ways(n) = ways(n-1) + ways(n-2), with one way to stand at the bottom and one way to reach the first step.
Iterate from the base cases upward, keeping only the previous two counts, so you build the answer in linear time and constant space.

stress.description: every value in the legal range
stress.note: small bounded domain; recurrence stays in safe integer range
